citations.narrative:
In February 2026, a member of the public was participating in an encounter with two otters when he was bitten on the
hand. According to the licensee, one of the otters grabbed onto the base of the guest's shirt. When the guest tried to
remove the otter, the otter bit his finger, resulting in a shallow puncture.
Failure to handle animals in a manner that minimizes risk can result in injury or be harmful to the health and well-being of
the animal or the public. During public exhibitions, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the
animal and to the public.
To be corrected from this day, April 23, 2026, forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.131(c)(1) 
   
Handling of animals. 
In February 2026, a member of the public was participating in an encounter with two otters when he was bitten on the 
hand. According to the licensee, one of the otters grabbed onto the base of the guest's shirt. When the guest tried to 
remove the otter, the otter bit his finger, resulting in a shallow puncture. 
 
Failure to handle animals in a manner that minimizes risk can result in injury or be harmful to the health and well-being of 
the animal or the public. During public exhibitions, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the 
animal and to the public.  
To be corrected from this day, April 23, 2026, forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
